title: Ignatius to the Philadelphians X
body: Since, according to your prayers, and the compassion which ye feel in Christ Jesus, it is reported to me that the church which is at Antioch in Syria possesses peace, it will become you, as a church of God, to elect a deacon to act as the ambassador of God [for you] to [the brethren there], that he may rejoice along with them when they are met together, and glorify the name [of God]. Blessed is he in Jesus Christ, who shall be deemed worthy of such a ministry; and ye too shall be glorified. And if ye are willing, it is not beyond your power to do this, for the sake of God; as also the nearest churches have sent, in some cases bishops, and in others presbyters and deacons.

source.label: Ignatius to the Philadelphians (shorter recension; trans. Roberts-Donaldson, 1885)

author: engine

witnesses.label: Middle Recension — Codex Mediceus Laurentianus 57.7 (10-11th cent)
Polycarp, Letter to the Philippians — cites Ignatius's letters by name
